Retrieve a list of agent entities sorted by creation time. Deprecated: some features such as agent sharing are not supported by this endpoint. Use the cursor-paginated `GET /v1/agents/pages` instead.

operations.summary: Create a agent that can be used within a conversation.
operations.description: Create a new agent giving it instructions, tools, description. The agent is then available to be used as a regular assistant in a conversation or as part of an agent pool from which it can be used.

Retrieve a page of agent entities. Unlike the deprecated `GET /v1/agents`, this endpoint paginates by opaque cursor and honors per-agent sharing, returning only agents the caller is authorized to see.
Given an agent, retrieve an agent entity with its attributes. The agent_version parameter can be an integer version number or a string alias.
Delete an agent entity.
Update an agent attributes and create a new version.
Switch the version of an agent.

operations.summary: List all versions of an agent.
operations.description: Retrieve all versions for a specific agent with full agent context. Supports pagination.

operations.summary: Retrieve a specific version of an agent.
operations.description: Get a specific agent version by version number.

operations.summary: List all aliases for an agent.
operations.description: Retrieve all version aliases for a specific agent.

operations.summary: Create or update an agent version alias.
operations.description: Create a new alias or update an existing alias to point to a specific version. Aliases are unique per agent and can be reassigned to different versions.
Delete an existing alias for an agent.
Retrieve a list of conversation entities sorted by creation time.

operations.summary: Create a conversation and append entries to it.
operations.description: Create a new conversation, using a base model or an agent and append entries. Completion and tool executions are run and the response is appended to the conversation.Use the returned conversation_id to continue the conversation.
Given a conversation_id retrieve a conversation entity with its attributes.

operations.summary: Append new entries to an existing conversation.
operations.description: Run completion on the history of the conversation and the user entries. Return the new created entries.
Delete a conversation given a conversation_id.

operations.summary: Retrieve all entries in a conversation.
operations.description: Given a conversation_id retrieve all the entries belonging to that conversation. The entries are sorted in the order they were appended, those can be messages, connectors or function_call.

operations.summary: Retrieve all messages in a conversation.
operations.description: Given a conversation_id retrieve all the messages belonging to that conversation. This is similar to retrieving all entries except we filter the messages only.

operations.summary: Restart a conversation starting from a given entry.
operations.description: Given a conversation_id and an id, recreate a conversation from this point and run completion. A new conversation is returned with the new entries returned.

operations.summary: Create a conversation and append entries to it.
operations.description: Create a new conversation, using a base model or an agent and append entries. Completion and tool executions are run and the response is appended to the conversation.Use the returned conversation_id to continue the conversation.

operations.summary: Append new entries to an existing conversation.
operations.description: Run completion on the history of the conversation and the user entries. Return the new created entries.

operations.summary: Restart a conversation starting from a given entry.
operations.description: Given a conversation_id and an id, recreate a conversation from this point and run completion. A new conversation is returned with the new entries returned.

operations.summary: List all libraries you have access to.
operations.description: List all libraries that you have created or have been shared with you.
Create a new Library, you will be marked as the owner and only you will have the possibility to share it with others. When first created this will only be accessible by you.

operations.summary: Detailed information about a specific Library.
operations.description: Given a library id, details information about that Library.
Given a library id, you can update the name and description.

operations.summary: Delete a library and all of it's document.
operations.description: Given a library id, deletes it together with all documents that have been uploaded to that library. Warning: the response will change from 200 (returning the deleted library) to 204 No Content in a future version.
Given a library id, you can update the name and description.

operations.summary: List documents in a given library.
operations.description: Given a library, lists the document that have been uploaded to that library.

Given a library, upload a new document to that library. It is queued for processing, it status will change it has been processed. The processing has to be completed in order be discoverable for the library search

operations.summary: Retrieve the metadata of a specific document.
operations.description: Given a library and a document in this library, you can retrieve the metadata of that document.

operations.summary: Update the metadata of a specific document.
operations.description: Given a library and a document in that library, update the name of that document.
Given a library and a document in that library, delete that document. The document will be deleted from the library and the search index.

operations.summary: Update the metadata of a specific document.
operations.description: Given a library and a document in that library, update the name and/or attributes of that document.

operations.summary: Retrieve the text content of a specific document.
operations.description: Given a library and a document in that library, you can retrieve the text content of that document if it exists. For documents like pdf, docx and pptx the text content results from our processing using Mistral OCR.

operations.summary: Retrieve the processing status of a specific document.
operations.description: Given a library and a document in that library, retrieve the processing status of that document.

operations.summary: Retrieve the signed URL of a specific document.
operations.description: Given a library and a document in that library, retrieve the signed URL of a specific document.The url will expire after 30 minutes and can be accessed by anyone with the link.

operations.summary: Retrieve the signed URL of text extracted from a given document.
operations.description: Given a library and a document in that library, retrieve the signed URL of text extracted. For documents that are sent to the OCR this returns the result of the OCR queries.
Given a library and a document in that library, reprocess that document, it will be billed again.

operations.summary: List all of the access to this library.
operations.description: Given a library, list all of the Entity that have access and to what level.

operations.summary: Create or update an access level.
operations.description: Given a library id, you can create or update the access level of an entity. You have to be owner of the library to share a library. An owner cannot change their own role. A library cannot be shared outside of the organization.

Given a library id, you can delete the access level of an entity. An owner cannot delete their own access. You have to be the owner of the library to delete an access other than yours. Warning: the response will change from 200 (returning the deleted sharing) to 204 No Content in a future version.
List all your custom connectors with keyset pagination and filters.
Create a new MCP connector. You can customize its visibility, url and auth type.

operations.summary: Get the auth URL for a connector.
operations.description: Get the OAuth2 authorization URL for a connector to initiate user authentication.

operations.summary: Share a private connector to the current workspace.
operations.description: Transfers ownership of a private user-owned connector to the current workspace, making it available to all workspace members. This action is irreversible: once shared, the connector belongs to the workspace and can no longer be used privately across other workspaces. Any authentication flows that rely on the original owner's identity (e.g. OAuth on-behalf-of) will be affected and must be reconfigured after sharing. Only the connector's creator can call this endpoint. Requires the ShareConnectorToWorkspace workspace permission.

Retrieve logs for a deployment (across all of its workers).

Use `after`/`before`/`order` on the first request to set the time range and sort order; for
the next pages pass the `cursor` from the previous response (it remembers the range and order).

Stream logs for a deployment (all of its workers) via SSE.

Resume cursor comes from the `Last-Event-ID` header or `last_event_id` query param (header wins)
and takes precedence over `after`; omit all to tail from the deployment start.

Stream

Retrieve logs for a workflow execution.

Use `after`/`before`/`order` on the first request to set the time range and sort order; for
the next pages pass the `cursor` from the previous response (it remembers the range and order).

Stream logs for a workflow execution via SSE.

Resume cursor comes from the `Last-Event-ID` header or `last_event_id` query param (header wins)
and takes precedence over `after`; omit all to tail from the execution start.

Get comprehensive metrics for a specific workflow.

Args:
    workflow_name: The name of the workflow type to get metrics for
    start_time: Optional start time filter (ISO 8601 format)
    end_time: Optional end time filter (ISO 8601 format)

Returns:
    WorkflowMetrics: Dictionary containing metrics:
        - execution_count: Total number of executions
        - success_count: Number of successful executions
        - error_count: Number of failed/terminated executions
        - average_latency_ms: Average execution duration in milliseconds
        - retry_rate: Proportion of workflows with retries
        - latency_over_time: Time-series data of execution durations

Example:
    GET /v1/workflows/MyWorkflow/metrics
    GET /v1/workflows/MyWorkflow/metrics?start_time=2025-01-01T00:00:00Z
    GET /v1/workflows/MyWorkflow/metrics?start_time=2025-01-01T00:00:00Z&end_time=2025-12-31T23:59:59Z
